name: square-blade-mcp
title: Square Payments + Billing
description: Square Payments + Billing API operations — payments, refunds, customers, cards, locations, orders, catalog, inventory, disputes, webhooks (payments-v1) and subscriptions, plans, invoices (billing-v1). PCI-safe card output, write-gated, confirm-gated for destructive ops.
tagline: POS, e-commerce, recurring billing, and invoicing through Square
author: groupthink-dev

env.name: SQUARE_ACCESS_TOKEN
env.description: Square personal access token from Developer Dashboard

env.name: SQUARE_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_KEY
env.description: Signing key for webhook HMAC verification

setup.help_text: Create a personal access token at Square → Developer Dashboard → Applications. Choose sandbox for testing or production for live payments.
setup.blurb: Square Payments + Billing API operations — payments, refunds, customers, cards, locations, orders, catalog, inventory, disputes, webhooks (payments-v1) and subscriptions, plans, invoices (billing-v1). PCI-safe card output, write-gated, confirm-gated for destructive ops.

highlights.text: Payments — charge, capture, cancel, refund with audit trail
Subscriptions & invoices — recurring billing, pause/resume, draft → publish → cancel
Customers & cards — manage customer profiles and saved cards (PCI-safe display)
Orders & catalog — line items, taxes, fulfilments, inventory counts
Multi-location — POS, online, physical locations with per-location reporting
Webhooks — single-call HMAC verification, event-type discovery
Write-gated — read-only by default, opt-in writes, confirm-gated destructives

readme:
### What it does

Square Blade MCP gives your AI agent access to Square Payments — payments, refunds, customers, saved cards, locations, orders, catalog, inventory, disputes, and webhooks. Your agent can investigate a customer issue, process a refund, look up an order's payment history, or verify an inbound webhook signature without leaving the conversation.

Ideal for retail, hospitality, and online merchants who want their AI to read and (selectively) write Square data.

### Sandbox-first

Defaults to Square's sandbox (`SQUARE_ENVIRONMENT=sandbox`). Explore the entire API surface — fake payments, fake customers, fake disputes — without touching real money. Switch to `production` only when you're ready to operate on live data.

### Token efficiency

Square's REST responses can include large nested objects (full card details, line items, fulfilment graphs). Each tool's formatter projects to pipe-delimited rows for lists and short labelled fields for detail views — money is rendered as `$29.00 USD`, dates as `2026-03-15 14:30`, null fields are dropped.

### PCI safety

Card output is restricted to last_4, brand, exp_month/year, and fingerprint. The full PAN never enters the conversation, even on detail tools. Webhook signature keys are masked in subscription detail views.

### Safety model

- **Reads** work immediately — list payments, view customers, fetch orders
- **Writes** require `SQUARE_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — create payments, update customers
- **Destructive operations** require both write enable AND `confirm=true` — cancel payment, delete customer, disable card, delete webhook subscription

### Why not the official Square MCP?

Square ships an official MCP that exposes a single meta-tool (`make_request`) and pushes parsing onto the model. That works for breadth but burns tokens and removes any chance of structured guardrails. This blade gives you 30 typed tools, formatted output, write/confirm gates, credential scrubbing, and PCI-safe card display. The two can coexist — use the official server as an escape hatch when you need an obscure endpoint.

scenarios.label: Investigate a customer payment dispute
scenarios.body: Look up the disputed payment, fetch the related customer profile and order, review past payments by this customer to assess their history, and gather the evidence the agent will need to respond to the dispute.

scenarios.label: Process a partial refund
scenarios.body: Fetch the original payment, calculate the refund amount, create the refund with an appropriate reason, and verify the customer is notified by inspecting recent webhooks.

scenarios.label: Audit recent inventory changes for a location
scenarios.body: List catalog items for the location, query inventory counts, identify items with low stock or unexpected drops, and surface the affected SKUs for restock.

scenarios.label: Onboard a new monthly subscriber
scenarios.body: Create a customer profile, save their card on file, then start a Square subscription against an existing plan variation. Confirm the first invoice is in DRAFT and ready to send.

scenarios.label: Pause and later resume a subscription
scenarios.body: Look up the subscription's current state, pause it (with confirm), then later resume it. Verify the pending pause/resume actions appear on the subscription record.
